text: Excuse me sir, how old were you when you made your first million? Million or billion? Billion. Billion? That was only recent. A million when I was 24. So you're a billionaire? Yes, fortunately I am. And what's your name? Tony Robbins. How would you not know that if you're at my home? And how did you get rich, man? What do you do? I have this point, 114 companies. We do $12 billion in business per year. Can I interview you for a minute real quick? Of course. Your best networking tip to the younger generation? I'll tell you what Jim Rohn taught me because the best advice I ever got. I was 17 years old. I went to one day and said, help me understand why we're broke. Doesn't seem fair that some school teacher makes in those days $35,000 a year and this billionaire hedge fund guy makes a billion in a year. And he said, Tony, listen to me. He said something I'll never forget. He goes, we're all equal as souls. You're right. But we're not equal in the marketplace. And he said, your job is very simple. You have to become more valuable. You have to do more for others than anybody else in the marketplace. So it's true when they say that life gives to givers and it takes from takers. I think life is always giving to us. It's our job to figure out what it is. I tell people you're rewarded in public for what you constantly practiced intensely in private. You also mastered the art of getting attention. What did you learn about building a personal brand to generate millions and billions of dollars? I would offer you a different thought on that. It's not just attention. The attention economy is already over. It's like the information economy. There's so much information. People are drowning in information. They're starving for wisdom and everybody's trying to get your attention. They've got advertisements on people's asses. It's on bananas. It's everywhere. Come on. What you need is to get results. There's no better marketing tool than results. Then you'll not just have attention. You'll have raving fans and those raving fans will build your business. Credibility kills all bad attitudes. That's the perfect way of saying it. How old are you now? 65, about to be 66. Tony, if me and you died tomorrow and you had one more message to leave with the younger generation, what would that be? There's three skills you got to have. The most important skills in life today. Skill number one is pattern recognition. That's what you're running around doing. What's the common pattern? What's the common belief system? Pattern recognition takes you out of fear. But the second skill is pattern utilization. If you look at somebody's good in finance, it's because they learn how to not see the pattern but use the pattern. But the third skill, the one you want to get to is pattern creation. That's when you come the greatest of all time in your particular category. That's how you get there. But I always tell people, we're not made to manage circumstances. We're made to be creators. We were created, designed to be creators. Become the creator of your own life. You got amazing advice, sir. Thank you. Thank you so much for your time, man. Nice meeting you. You're a massive inspiration. Thank you so much. Blessings to you.
